title: Odontites asperus
taxonomy: Odontites asperus is classified within the family Orobanchaceae. This classification places Odontites asperus as a member of the broomrape family, a group of flowering plants that are known to be hemiparasitic. The genus Odontites is further grouped within the tribe Orobancheae, which includes other broomrape species. Odontites asperus is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe, Africa, and Asia. Its classification is primarily based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other Orobanchaceae species.
uses: Odontites asperus is a plant species, however, there is limited information on its medicinal uses. It is primarily known for its ornamental value, being used as a decorative plant in gardens and landscapes. While some plant species within the Odontites genus have been studied for their potential medicinal properties, specific uses for Odontites asperus are not well-documented. Further research would be needed to determine any potential benefits or applications of the plant in medicine. More studies are required to understand the full potential of Odontites asperus.

parts: Odontites asperus, also known as downy toothwort, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine. side_effects: Odontites asperus is a plant species that may cause side effects when ingested due to the presence of toxic compounds. Ingestion of this plant can lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The toxic compounds found in Odontites asperus can also cause irritation to the skin and mucous membranes. If ingested in large quantities, it may cause more severe symptoms, including abdominal pain and respiratory distress. As with any potentially toxic plant, handling and ingestion of Odontites asperus should be avoided.
